signals.reasoning: BTC is stabilizing after the prior flush, but the macro regime is still RED and the tape is not clean enough to justify a fresh directional bet here. Best to leave the majors neutral.
ETH is behaving like a stabilization bounce rather than a fresh breakout or breakdown. With macro still weak, the cleaner edge is elsewhere.
SOL still looks more like a post-liquidation reset than a clean continuation trade in either direction. That keeps it on the neutral list.

0G remains the strongest squeeze-long candidate on the sheet. Funding is still materially negative while price and open interest are rising together, which keeps the trend-supported squeeze thesis alive even after the initial run.
STBL is trending up with crowded-long pressure, but that makes it more awkward than attractive right here. It is not broken enough to short and not clean enough to buy.
DOOD is too thin and too noisy to trust for a fresh entry. The rebound is real, but the execution quality is not.
AVNT has some rebound potential after the prior liquidation flush, but the setup is still mixed rather than clean. It is interesting, but not strong enough to promote above watch-only.
NIL remains too thin and too flat to offer a meaningful edge. No need to force it.
WCT looks more like a short-covering bounce than a durable trend turn, but the downside is not clean enough to press either. Neutral is the right call.
STABLE is still one of the few names with real squeeze fuel. Funding remains negative while price is holding strong, so it stays a reduced-size long candidate even after the sharp move.
GOAT is still damaged, but it is already too far gone and too unstable to trust as a fresh short. Weak structure alone is not enough.
